generated_assessment.reasoning: This 55-year-old male with very high openness (86th pct) and agreeableness (78th pct) but extremely low conscientiousness (12th pct) and extraversion (12th pct) shows a baseline score (-237) already aligned with the adversary's goal of decreasing alliance commitment support. The T1 atomic fear appeal targeting personal safety over hours is a weak, impersonal mechanism unlikely to shift someone whose strong social trust (88th pct) and emotional stability (30th pct stress vulnerability) would resist a simple fear-based reinforcement. The very low positive emotions (2nd pct) and low activity/excitement-seeking further limit engagement with a single fear message, so movement is minimal—likely no more than a test-retest fluctuation of 2 points downward.

attack_vector_spec.attack_leaf: Political_Opinion_Cybermanipulation_Ontology > Primary_Axis > Attack_Family > Claim_Frame_And_Narrative_Manipulation > Emotional_And_Moral_Engineering > Fear_Appeals > Personal_Safety_Fear_Appeal

attack_context.baseline_vs_goal: ALIGNED — baseline already points in the adversarial direction; reinforce it
attack_context.persuasion_goal: intensify and solidify an already negative opinion on Alliance Commitment Support — push it further in the negative direction
attack_context.motivational_lever: intensify and solidify the existing position, supply confirmatory anecdotes, and harden the boundary against opposing arguments
attack_context.emotional_register: threat salience, doubt, systemic-failure framing, loss framing, and elite distrust cues
attack_context.issue_frame: defense and national security, related policy debates, and societal impacts
